corpus.researchSummary: Ramp is a U.S.-based fintech company that provides corporate cards, expense management, bill pay, and related finance automation software for businesses.

geography.value: Primarily U.S.-based, with additional offices in Miami and San Francisco
geography.reasoning: Wikipedia states Ramp is headquartered in New York City and has additional offices in Miami and San Francisco.

jobTitles.value: CFO, VP of Finance, Controller, Finance Manager, Accounts Payable Manager
jobTitles.reasoning: These titles are inferred from the finance-team focus of the product. They are not explicitly listed in the captured evidence.

valueProp.value: Helps businesses save time and money by unifying spend management in one platform.
valueProp.reasoning: The official site states Ramp helps businesses save time and money with corporate cards, expense management, bill pay, and more in one place.

uniqueEdge.value: Unified spend management platform combining corporate cards, expense management, and bill pay.
uniqueEdge.reasoning: Ramp's core differentiation in the captured evidence is its all-in-one finance automation and spend management platform.

companyName.reasoning: The company is identified as Ramp Business Corporation and branded as Ramp across the captured sources.

businessModel.value: B2B fintech platform for corporate cards and spend management software.
businessModel.reasoning: Captured sources describe Ramp as a financial technology company offering corporate charge cards, expense management, and bill-payment software.

marketProblem.value: Fragmented spend management and manual finance workflows.
marketProblem.reasoning: The product is framed as helping finance teams control company spend, implying the problem of fragmented or hard-to-control spend workflows.

brandPositioning.value: Modern spend management and finance automation platform for businesses.
brandPositioning.reasoning: Captured sources consistently position Ramp as a spend management platform that helps finance teams control company spend.

coreDeliverables.value: Corporate charge cards, expense management, bill-payment software, and spend management controls.
coreDeliverables.reasoning: The support overview and company description indicate these are the core product deliverables.

industryVertical.reasoning: Wikipedia explicitly describes Ramp as a financial technology company.

productDescription.value: Ramp offers corporate cards, expense management, bill pay, and finance automation software for businesses.
productDescription.reasoning: This is directly supported by the company description in the captured evidence.

headquartersLocation.value: New York City, New York, United States
headquartersLocation.reasoning: Wikipedia explicitly states the company is headquartered in New York City.

desiredTransformation.value: Move to a single system for controlling spend, managing expenses, and paying bills.
desiredTransformation.reasoning: Ramp's official positioning emphasizes one platform for corporate cards, expense management, bill pay, and savings.

primaryIcpDescription.value: Businesses and finance teams that want to control company spend and automate expense and bill-pay workflows.
primaryIcpDescription.reasoning: Ramp is described as a spend management platform that helps finance teams control company spend.

situationBeforeBuying.value: Finance teams rely on separate tools and manual processes to manage spend, expenses, and bills.
situationBeforeBuying.reasoning: This is a synthesis of the third-party review and Ramp's own positioning. It is not stated verbatim in the captured evidence.

artifact.title: Ramp GTM Research
artifact.markdown:
## Deep Research

Ramp is a U.S.-based fintech company that provides corporate cards, expense management, bill pay, and related finance automation software for businesses.

### Evidence Highlights
- Ramp is a financial technology company that offers corporate charge cards, expense management, and bill-payment software. (Ramp Wikipedia page)
- Ramp is headquartered in New York City. (Ramp Wikipedia page)
- Ramp provides a platform that helps businesses save time and money with corporate cards, expense management, bill pay, and more. (Ramp official site)
- Ramp includes corporate cards as part of its finance automation platform. (Ramp overview)
- Ramp offers expense management capabilities for finance teams. (Ramp overview)

### Sources
- Ramp overview (https://support.ramp.com/hc/en-us/articles/4417685250195-Ramp-overview): Primary product overview describing Ramp's platform and feature set.
- Ramp support center (https://support.ramp.com/hc/en-us): Confirms Ramp operates a support center for customers and documents product scope.
- Ramp official site (https://rampnetwork.com/): Official company site used to identify brand and corporate positioning.
- Ramp Wikipedia page (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ramp_(company)): Independent company overview with headquarters, business description, and corporate context.
- Ramp review by Exbo Group (https://www.exbogroup.com/article/reviewing-ramp-com-a-smart-way-to-improve-business-finances): Third-party summary of Ramp's positioning and value proposition.
- Ramp App Store listing (https://apps.apple.com/us/app/ramp/id1628197245): Confirms Ramp offers a mobile app and indicates customer-facing product availability.
- Ramp YouTube video (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yazRO0jRTag): Captured media source associated with Ramp's product and company messaging.

## Deep Research

Ramp is a U.S.-based fintech company that provides corporate cards, expense management, bill pay, and related finance automation software for businesses.

### Evidence Highlights
- Ramp is a financial technology company that offers corporate charge cards, expense management, and bill-payment software. (Ramp Wikipedia page)
- Ramp is headquartered in New York City. (Ramp Wikipedia page)
- Ramp provides a platform that helps businesses save time and money with corporate cards, expense management, bill pay, and more. (Ramp official site)
- Ramp includes corporate cards as part of its finance automation platform. (Ramp overview)
- Ramp offers expense management capabilities for finance teams. (Ramp overview)

### Sources
- Ramp overview (https://support.ramp.com/hc/en-us/articles/4417685250195-Ramp-overview): Primary product overview describing Ramp's platform and feature set.
- Ramp support center (https://support.ramp.com/hc/en-us): Confirms Ramp operates a support center for customers and documents product scope.
- Ramp official site (https://rampnetwork.com/): Official company site used to identify brand and corporate positioning.
- Ramp Wikipedia page (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ramp_(company)): Independent company overview with headquarters, business description, and corporate context.
- Ramp review by Exbo Group (https://www.exbogroup.com/article/reviewing-ramp-com-a-smart-way-to-improve-business-finances): Third-party summary of Ramp's positioning and value proposition.
- Ramp App Store listing (https://apps.apple.com/us/app/ramp/id1628197245): Confirms Ramp offers a mobile app and indicates customer-facing product availability.
- Ramp YouTube video (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yazRO0jRTag): Captured media source associated with Ramp's product and company messaging.

insights.insight:
Ramp is a financial technology company that offers corporate charge cards, expense management, and bill-payment software.
> "Ramp Business Corporation is an American multinational financial technology company that offers corporate charge cards, expense management, and bill-payment software."
— Ramp Wikipedia page (confidence: 95)

Ramp is headquartered in New York City.
> "The company is headquartered in New York City with additional offices in Miami and San Francisco."
— Ramp Wikipedia page (confidence: 95)

Ramp provides a platform that helps businesses save time and money with corporate cards, expense management, bill pay, and more.
> "Ramp helps businesses save time and money with corporate cards, expense management, bill pay, and more—all in one."
— Ramp official site (confidence: 90)

Ramp includes corporate cards as part of its finance automation platform.
> "Ramp is a spend management platform that helps finance teams control company spend."
— Ramp overview (confidence: 88)

Ramp offers expense management capabilities for finance teams.
> "Ramp is a spend management platform that helps finance teams control company spend."
— Ramp overview (confidence: 88)

Ramp offers bill pay or accounts payable functionality.
> "...offers corporate charge cards, expense management, and bill-payment software."
— Ramp Wikipedia page (confidence: 95)

Ramp provides customer support documentation and a support center for users.
> "Ramp Help Center"
— Ramp support center (confidence: 80)

Ramp has a mobile app available in the Apple App Store.
> "Ramp on the App Store"
— Ramp App Store listing (confidence: 80)

artifact.title: Ramp GTM Research
artifact.markdown:
## Deep Research

Ramp is a U.S.-based fintech company that provides corporate cards, expense management, bill pay, and related finance automation software for businesses.

### Evidence Highlights
- Ramp is a financial technology company that offers corporate charge cards, expense management, and bill-payment software. (Ramp Wikipedia page)
- Ramp is headquartered in New York City. (Ramp Wikipedia page)
- Ramp provides a platform that helps businesses save time and money with corporate cards, expense management, bill pay, and more. (Ramp official site)
- Ramp includes corporate cards as part of its finance automation platform. (Ramp overview)
- Ramp offers expense management capabilities for finance teams. (Ramp overview)

### Sources
- Ramp overview (https://support.ramp.com/hc/en-us/articles/4417685250195-Ramp-overview): Primary product overview describing Ramp's platform and feature set.
- Ramp support center (https://support.ramp.com/hc/en-us): Confirms Ramp operates a support center for customers and documents product scope.
- Ramp official site (https://rampnetwork.com/): Official company site used to identify brand and corporate positioning.
- Ramp Wikipedia page (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ramp_(company)): Independent company overview with headquarters, business description, and corporate context.
- Ramp review by Exbo Group (https://www.exbogroup.com/article/reviewing-ramp-com-a-smart-way-to-improve-business-finances): Third-party summary of Ramp's positioning and value proposition.
- Ramp App Store listing (https://apps.apple.com/us/app/ramp/id1628197245): Confirms Ramp offers a mobile app and indicates customer-facing product availability.
- Ramp YouTube video (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yazRO0jRTag): Captured media source associated with Ramp's product and company messaging.
